# @meli/deprecation

Easily show deprecation warning messages to your consumers.

## Description

This module exposes a simple way to create and show deprecation warnings to your consumers.

## Install

```sh
npm install --save @meli/deprecation
```

## Usage

### `createDeprecationLogger`

Function to create the desired deprecation logger function. It receives your module's name and a set of options.

> [!NOTE]
>
> Warnings will **NOT** be shown in production environments. These are only intended for development.

#### `createDeprecationLogger` Syntax

```js
createDeprecationLogger(namespace);
createDeprecationLogger(namespace, options);
```

#### `createDeprecationLogger` Parameters

- `namespace:` `string` - Name of the module generating the deprecation warning to be displayed in the warnings. You can create many deprecation loggers for the same module. By default, this namespace will be used as the module's name to filter the stack trace from showing the invoking module's traces.
- `options:` [`WarnOptions`](#createdeprecationlogger-options) (optional) - Object with options to customize how the warning will be displayed.

##### `createDeprecationLogger` Options

| Parameter          |   Type    | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ------------------ | :-------: | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `once`             | `boolean` | Show warning only once, no matter how many times the function is called. Defaults to `true`.                                                                                                                            |
| `moduleName`       | `string`  | Name of the module throwing the warning, as installed in `node_modules`. This is used to filter the stack trace to show only the relevant lines. If not provided, the namespace will be used to filter the stack trace. |
| `showStackTrace`   | `boolean` | Show complete stack trace to indicate where the warning was triggered. Defaults to `false`.                                                                                                                             |
| `directDependency` | `boolean` | Show warning only if deprecated code is being accessed directly by the running project, instead of being accessed by another dependency. Defaults to `true`.                                                            |

---

#### `createDeprecationLogger` Return value

The `logDeprecated` function that allows to log a deprecation warning.

##### `logDeprecated` Syntax

```js
const logDeprecated = createDeprecationLogger('my-lib-name');

logDeprecated(message);
logDeprecated(message, options);
```

##### `logDeprecated` Parameters

- `message:` `string` - The deprecation message to display.
- `options:` [`LoggerOptions`](#logdeprecated-options) (optional) - An object with options to customize how the warning is displayed.

###### `logDeprecated` Options

| Parameter     | Type            | Description                                                                                      |
| ------------- | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `customStack` | `Array<string>` | Custom stack trace to display in the warning, instead of the automatically captured stack trace. |

## Examples

### Simple warning

```js
import { createDeprecationLogger } from '@meli/deprecation';

const logDeprecated = createDeprecationLogger('my-lib-name', { once: true });

logDeprecated(
  'This module is deprecated. Please migrate to the new one. Link to new module: https://my-new-module.com',
);
```

### Warning using a custom stack trace

In some cases, such as logging deprecation warnings inside React components, the default stack trace may include a lot of React internal calls, making it difficult for consumers to find the actual source in their code.

By using the `customStack` option, you can pass a stack trace that is more relevant for your users.

```js
import { captureOwnerStack } from 'react';

import { createDeprecationLogger } from '@meli/deprecation';

const logDeprecated = createDeprecationLogger('my-lib-name');

const MyComponent = () => {
  // The customStack needs to be an array
  const stack = captureOwnerStack().split('\n');

  // Clean the stack if needed
  stack.shift();

  logDeprecated('This is deprecated, please use the alternative method.', {
    // Pass a custom stack trace (captured outside of React's internals)
    customStack: stack,
  });

  return null;
};
```

> [!TIP]
>
> This module respects Node's flags [--trace-deprecation](https://nodejs.org/docs/latest/api/cli.html#--trace-deprecation), [--trace-warnings](https://nodejs.org/docs/latest/api/cli.html#--trace-warnings), [--no-deprecation](https://nodejs.org/docs/latest/api/cli.html#--no-deprecation) and [--no-warnings](https://nodejs.org/docs/latest/api/cli.html#--no-warnings).
